title: penstemon procerus var. brachyanthus
intro: Penstemon procerus var. brachyanthus, also known as the rock penstemon, is a variety of flowering plant in the plantain family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and to treat a variety of health issues, such as fever and rheumatism. The health benefits of Penstemon procerus var. brachyanthus include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, making it a valuable plant for herbal remedies. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used in salves and poultices to treat skin conditions and wounds.
